name: Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop
name_original: De Cardeña al Embalse de las Tejoneras — circular por el Parque Natura Sierra de Andújar
description: Jog this moderate 6.5-mile circular route from Cardeña to the Embalse de las Tejoneras, enjoying dehesa landscapes and Mediterranean forest.
region_intro: Jogging in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by its natural park, featuring river meanders, reservoirs, and varied terrain suitable for different running styles. These routes provide opportunities for wildlife spotting in a natural setting. The area includes both challenging trails and more accessible jogging routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro. Best jogging routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro The most popular jogging route is Yeguas River Meander - Obejo Bridge Loop, a 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the river, offering scenic views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route circles the reservoir, providing a mix of open spaces and natural surroundings. Local runners also love the Cardeña y El Cerezo – circular through the Cardeña y Montoro Sierra Natural Park, a 13.9 miles (22.3 km) trail leading through the Sierra Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Jogging in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro is defined by river meanders, reservoirs, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for easy jogging routes as well as more difficult running challenges. The routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: There are over 20 running routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro, offering a variety of distances and difficulties. These trails are popular among local runners, with more than 100 people having used komoot to explore the area.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: Yes, the region offers options for easy jogging routes. While many trails present a moderate to difficult challenge, there is at least one route specifically categorized as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a lighter run. You can find more details on komoot to filter by difficulty.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: Running routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro vary significantly. You can find shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1303820764/cardena-and-tejoneras-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 10.5 km (6.5 miles) and takes around 1 hour 15 minutes. Longer, more challenging runs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001078236/cardena-y-el-cerezo-circular-through-the-cardena-y-montoro-sierra-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña y El Cerezo – circular through the Cardeña y Montoro Sierra Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 22.3 km (13.9 miles) and take over 2 hours 30 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304473999/yeguas-river-meander-obejo-bridge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yeguas River Meander - Obejo Bridge Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1303820764/cardena-and-tejoneras-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while running in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro Natural Park is known for its rich biodiversity. Running through the park's natural settings, especially along river meanders and reservoir areas, provides excellent opportunities for wildlife spotting. Keep an eye out for local fauna as you enjoy your run.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails?
A: The running trails in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro offer diverse scenery, characterized by river meanders, reservoirs, and the natural park landscapes. You'll encounter a mix of open spaces, forested areas, and potentially viewpoints offering panoramic vistas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the running routes?
A: Yes, several points of interest are located within or near the running routes. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo Bridge over the Yeguas River&lt;/a&gt;, or charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4289170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Constitución de Cardeña&lt;/a&gt;. These add cultural and scenic value to your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro, with an average score of 1.7 stars. Runners often praise the diverse landscape, the opportunities for wildlife spotting, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels, from easy jogs to challenging runs through the natural park.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Marmolejo Spa?
A: Yes, there are routes that start from or pass near the Marmolejo Spa. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa loop from Balneario de Marmolejo (museo)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.8 km (8.6 miles) route, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa – Pilgrim's Viewpoint loop from Balneario de Marmolejo (museo)&lt;/a&gt; covers 22.6 km (14 miles).

Q: Is Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro suitable for family running outings?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the presence of easier trails and the natural park setting make it possible for families to enjoy running together. Look for shorter, less challenging routes, especially those that might pass by points of interest or offer gentle terrain, to ensure an enjoyable experience for all family members.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The best time to go running in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions. Summers can be very hot, so if running during this season, it's advisable to go early in the morning or late in the evening. Winters are generally mild, but some trails might be wet after rain.
tips: Reservoir that is located in the vicinity of Cardeña and that is surrounded by devesas typical of this western area of the Sierra d'Andújar Natural Park.

Cardeña is a charming municipality located in the province of Córdoba, in the heart of the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park. 
In addition, Cardeña is known for its rich gastronomy and natural surroundings, making it a perfect destination for rural tourism.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Cardeña Village. While specific parking areas for this exact jogging route aren't detailed, you'll likely find parking options within or on the outskirts of Cardeña, which serves as a common starting point for routes in the area. Look for designated parking near the village center or trailheads.

faqs.question: Is this a circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, as indicated by its name: "From Cardeña to the Embalse de las Tejoneras — circular through the Parque Natura Sierra de Andújar." You'll start and end in the same general area, making it convenient for parking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring mostly accessible paths. You can expect a mix of natural trails, possibly some unpaved tracks or gravel sections. The area is characterized by gentle undulations and rolling hills, so you'll encounter both inclines and flatter stretches. Some parts might require sure-footedness due to rocky or uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of the Tejoneras Reservoir, surrounded by 'dehesas'—cultural landscapes with scattered oak trees over pastureland. The route also passes through well-preserved Mediterranean forest and scrubland, offering a rich natural backdrop. The starting point is near Cardeña Village.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar is renowned for its biodiversity. While jogging, you might spot deer, wild boars, foxes, and various bird species. It's also one of the last strongholds of the critically endangered Iberian lynx, though sightings are rare. Dawn or dusk are often the best times for wildlife observation.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, natural parks in Andalusia allow dogs, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check for specific signage or local regulations at the trailhead or within the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar to confirm any restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit and jog this route is typically during spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler with potential for rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not typically need permits or pay an entrance fee to access the general trails within the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar or the adjacent Parque Natural Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro. These are public natural spaces. However, always respect park regulations and stay on marked paths.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Transandalus, the Camino del Robledillo, and the Camino de Piedra Empiná during your jog.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' difficulty for jogging. While it features accessible paths, the varied terrain with gentle undulations and rolling hills means it's best suited for individuals with a good fitness level. Beginners might find it challenging but achievable with preparation.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cardeña Village, the starting point, is known for rural tourism. You'll find options for accommodation and local gastronomy there, making it a convenient base for your visit.

faqs.question: When is the best time of day to experience the wildlife on the trail?
faqs.answer: To maximize your chances of spotting wildlife, such as deer or wild boars, consider jogging during dawn or dusk. These are typically the most active times for many animals in the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar.
